1	package escalation
2	
3	import (
4		"encoding/json"
5		"fmt"

8		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
9	)

11	// SuccessStatus mirrors agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess. The agent package's
12	// TestEscalatableStatusesMatchAgentVocab guard catches drift if either side
13	// renames. Defined locally so escalation does not import agent.
14	const SuccessStatus = "success"
15	
16	// EscalatableStatuses is the set of executor status strings that warrant
17	// retrying with a higher tier. Mirrors a subset of agent.ExecuteBeadStatus*
18	// strings; the agent package has a TestEscalatableStatusesMatchAgentVocab
19	// guard (see agent/tier_escalation_alignment_test.go) to catch drift.
20	var EscalatableStatuses = map[string]bool{
21		"execution_failed":             true,
22		"post_run_check_failed":        true,
23		"land_conflict":                true,
24		"structural_validation_failed": true,
25	}
26	
27	// ShouldEscalate reports whether status warrants escalating to the next tier.
28	// Structural failures (e.g. validation errors) do not escalate because a
29	// smarter model cannot fix a malformed prompt or corrupted bead state.
30	func ShouldEscalate(status string) bool {
31		return EscalatableStatuses[status]
32	}
33	
34	// ProviderCooldownDuration is how long an unhealthy harness is skipped before
35	// re-probing. Five minutes gives most transient errors time to resolve without
36	// locking out a provider for too long.
37	//
38	// Per-harness cooldown tracking moved to the upstream agent service in v0.8.0:
39	// callers record failures via svc.RecordRouteAttempt and read cooldown state
40	// via svc.RouteStatus's RouteCandidateStatus.Healthy (ddx-7bc0c8d5).
41	const ProviderCooldownDuration = 5 * time.Minute
42	
43	// FormatTierAttemptBody formats the body of a tier-attempt bead event.
44	func FormatTierAttemptBody(tier, harness, model, probeResult, detail string) string {
45		body := fmt.Sprintf("tier=%s harness=%s model=%s", tier, harness, model)
46		if probeResult != "" {
47			body += " probe=" + probeResult
48		}
49		if detail != "" {
50			body += "\n" + detail
51		}
52		return body
53	}
54	
55	// EscalationWinningExhausted is the sentinel value written into the
56	// winning_tier field of an escalation-summary body when the escalation loop
57	// ran through every eligible tier without producing a successful attempt.
58	const EscalationWinningExhausted = "exhausted"
59	
60	// TierAttemptRecord is one row of an escalation trace. It captures the
61	// tier/harness/model that was tried, the status that attempt returned, and
62	// the cost/duration the harness reported for that attempt. Skipped tiers
63	// (no viable provider) are recorded with zero cost and zero duration.
64	type TierAttemptRecord struct {
65		Tier       string  `json:"tier"`
66		Harness    string  `json:"harness,omitempty"`
67		Model      string  `json:"model,omitempty"`
68		Status     string  `json:"status"`
69		CostUSD    float64 `json:"cost_usd"`
70		DurationMS int64   `json:"duration_ms"`
71	}
72	
73	// EscalationSummary is the structured body of a kind:escalation-summary bead
74	// event. It captures the entire escalation trace so an operator can diagnose
75	// which tiers were tried, which one won (if any), and how much the path cost.
76	type EscalationSummary struct {
77		TiersAttempted []TierAttemptRecord `json:"tiers_attempted"`
78		WinningTier    string              `json:"winning_tier"`
79		TotalCostUSD   float64             `json:"total_cost_usd"`
80		WastedCostUSD  float64             `json:"wasted_cost_usd"`
81	}
82	
83	// BuildEscalationSummary computes the summary body from the ordered list of
84	// attempts. winningTier is the string of the tier whose attempt succeeded;
85	// pass "" when the escalation was exhausted, in which case winning_tier is
86	// set to EscalationWinningExhausted. Total cost is the sum of all attempt
87	// costs; wasted cost is the sum of attempts whose status is not
88	// SuccessStatus.
89	func BuildEscalationSummary(attempts []TierAttemptRecord, winningTier string) EscalationSummary {
90		out := EscalationSummary{
91			TiersAttempted: append([]TierAttemptRecord{}, attempts...),
92			WinningTier:    winningTier,
93		}
94		if out.WinningTier == "" {
95			out.WinningTier = EscalationWinningExhausted
96		}
97		for _, a := range attempts {
98			out.TotalCostUSD += a.CostUSD
99			if a.Status != SuccessStatus {
100				out.WastedCostUSD += a.CostUSD
101			}
102		}
103		return out
104	}
105	
106	// BeadEventAppender records append-only evidence events on a bead. Mirrors
107	// agent.BeadEventAppender so escalation can append events without importing
108	// agent. *bead.Store satisfies both interfaces.
109	type BeadEventAppender interface {
110		AppendEvent(id string, event bead.BeadEvent) error
111	}
112	
113	// AppendEscalationSummaryEvent writes a kind:escalation-summary event to the
114	// bead with a JSON body describing the tier escalation trace. It is a
115	// best-effort operation — errors from the underlying store are returned so
116	// callers can log them, but callers typically ignore the error so telemetry
117	// failures never abort the main execute-bead flow.
118	func AppendEscalationSummaryEvent(appender BeadEventAppender, beadID, actor string, attempts []TierAttemptRecord, winningTier string, createdAt time.Time) error {
119		if appender == nil || beadID == "" {
120			return nil
121		}
122		summary := BuildEscalationSummary(attempts, winningTier)
123		body, err := json.Marshal(summary)
124		if err != nil {
125			return err
126		}
127		return appender.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
128			Kind:      "escalation-summary",
129			Summary:   fmt.Sprintf("winning_tier=%s attempts=%d total_cost_usd=%.4f wasted_cost_usd=%.4f", summary.WinningTier, len(attempts), summary.TotalCostUSD, summary.WastedCostUSD),
130			Body:      string(body),
131			Actor:     actor,
132			Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
133			CreatedAt: createdAt,
134		})
135	}

317				if verdict == VerdictRequestChanges {
318					report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewRequestChanges
319					report.Detail = "pre-land review: REQUEST_CHANGES"
320				} else {
321					report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewBlock
322					report.Detail = "pre-land review: BLOCK"
323				}
324				if verdict == VerdictBlock && classification.Class == ReviewFindingClassMalfunction {
325					report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewMalfunction
326					report.Detail = "pre-land review: " + ReviewFindingClassMalfunction
327				}
328				c.appendCandidateReviewClassifiedEvent(beadID, report, reviewResult, classification.Class, classification.Reason)
329				if !classification.AutomatedRepairEligible || c.Repair == nil {
330					return AttemptCycleResult{Report: report}, nil
331				}
332				if repairCycles >= c.maxRepairCycles() {
333					report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusRepairCycleExhausted
334					report.OutcomeReason = ExecuteBeadStatusRepairCycleExhausted
335					report.Detail = "pre-land repair: " + ExecuteBeadStatusRepairCycleExhausted
336					return AttemptCycleResult{Report: report}, nil
337				}
338				prompt := BuildRepairPrompt(repairPromptInput(beadID, candidate, reviewResult, repairCycles+1))
339				c.appendRepairCycleStartedEvent(beadID, candidate, repairCycles+1, classification.Class)
340				c.recordCandidateCycleState(candidate, CandidateCycleState{
341					Active:       true,
342					Phase:        "repair",
343					CandidateRef: candidate.Report.CandidateRef,
344					CandidateRev: candidate.Report.ResultRev,
345					CycleIndex:   candidate.CycleIndex,
346					RepairActive: true,
347				})
348				repaired, repairErr := c.Repair.Repair(ctx, candidate, prompt)
349				if repairErr != nil {
350					report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
351					report.Detail = "pre-land repair: " + repairErr.Error()
352					report.Error = repairErr.Error()
353					return AttemptCycleResult{Report: report}, nil
354				}
355				repairCycles++
356				candidate = normalizeRepairedCandidate(candidate, repaired)
357				if candidate.Report.Status != ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess {
358					return AttemptCycleResult{Report: candidate.Report}, nil
359				}
360				continue
361			default:
362				report := candidate.Report
363				report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewMalfunction
364				report.Detail = "pre-land review: malformed verdict"
365				report.ReviewVerdict = strings.TrimSpace(reviewResult.Verdict)
366				report.ReviewRationale = strings.TrimSpace(reviewResult.Rationale)
367				c.appendCandidateReviewClassifiedEvent(beadID, report, reviewResult, ReviewFindingClassMalfunction, "malformed review verdict")
368				return AttemptCycleResult{Report: report}, nil
369			}
370			break
371		}
372	
373		landed, err := c.Lander.Land(ctx, candidate)
374		if err != nil {
375			return AttemptCycleResult{Report: candidate.Report}, err
376		}
377	
378		// Propagate candidate metadata to the landed report when the lander does
379		// not set them (e.g. in tests that return candidate.Report directly).
380		if landed.CandidateRef == "" && candidate.Report.CandidateRef != "" {
381			landed.CandidateRef = candidate.Report.CandidateRef
382			landed.CycleIndex = candidate.Report.CycleIndex
383		}
384	
385		result := AttemptCycleResult{Report: landed, Landed: true}
386	
387		// Clean up the candidate ref after successful landing. For any non-success
388		// outcome (preserved, conflicted, parked, manual) the ref is retained so
389		// operators can inspect the candidate commit after the worktree is removed.
390		if c.RefStore != nil && c.ProjectRoot != "" && landed.CandidateRef != "" {
391			if !ShouldRetainCandidateRef(landed.Status) {
392				for _, ref := range pinnedRefs {
393					_ = c.RefStore.UnpinCandidateRef(c.ProjectRoot, ref)
394				}
395			}
396		}
397	
398		return result, nil
399	}

582	// WriteWorktreeCandidateCycleState updates cleanup metadata and, when a
583	// matching run-state exists, the per-attempt run-state snapshot for a live
584	// candidate-cycle worktree.
585	func WriteWorktreeCandidateCycleState(projectRoot, wtPath, attemptID string, state CandidateCycleState) error {
586		meta, err := ReadExecutionCleanupMetadata(wtPath)
587		if err != nil && !errors.Is(err, os.ErrNotExist) {
588			return fmt.Errorf("write candidate cycle state: read metadata: %w", err)
589		}
590		meta.WorktreePath = firstNonEmpty(meta.WorktreePath, wtPath)
591		meta.ProjectRoot = firstNonEmpty(meta.ProjectRoot, projectRoot)
592		meta.AttemptID = firstNonEmpty(meta.AttemptID, attemptID)
593		applyCandidateCycleStateToMetadata(&meta, state)
594		if err := WriteExecutionCleanupMetadata(wtPath, meta); err != nil {
595			return err
596		}
597		if projectRoot == "" || meta.AttemptID == "" {
598			return nil
599		}
600		states, err := ReadRunStates(projectRoot)
601		if err != nil {
602			return nil
603		}
604		for i := range states {
605			current := states[i]
606			if current.AttemptID != meta.AttemptID {
607				continue
608			}
609			applyCandidateCycleStateToRunState(&current, state)
610			return WriteRunState(projectRoot, current)
611		}
612		return nil
613	}

615	// MarkWorktreeActiveCycle sets the ActiveCandidateCycle flag in the worktree's
616	// cleanup metadata. The cleanup manager preserves any worktree with this flag
617	// set so it is never deleted mid-cycle before the coordinator disposes it.
618	func MarkWorktreeActiveCycle(wtPath string) error {
619		return WriteWorktreeCandidateCycleState("", wtPath, "", CandidateCycleState{
620			Active: true,
621			Phase:  "active",
622		})
623	}

625	// ClearWorktreeActiveCycle clears the ActiveCandidateCycle flag in the
626	// worktree's cleanup metadata after the coordinator reaches terminal disposition.
627	func ClearWorktreeActiveCycle(wtPath string) error {
628		meta, err := ReadExecutionCleanupMetadata(wtPath)
629		if err != nil {
630			if errors.Is(err, os.ErrNotExist) {
631				return nil
632			}
633			return fmt.Errorf("clear active cycle: read metadata: %w", err)
634		}
635		meta.ActiveCandidateCycle = false
636		meta.CandidateCyclePhase = ""
637		meta.ReviewActive = false
638		meta.RepairActive = false
639		return WriteExecutionCleanupMetadata(wtPath, meta)
640	}
641	
642	func applyCandidateCycleStateToMetadata(meta *ExecutionCleanupMetadata, state CandidateCycleState) {
643		meta.ActiveCandidateCycle = state.Active
644		meta.CandidateCyclePhase = state.Phase
645		meta.CandidateRef = state.CandidateRef
646		meta.CandidateRev = state.CandidateRev
647		meta.CycleIndex = state.CycleIndex
648		meta.ReviewActive = state.ReviewActive
649		meta.RepairActive = state.RepairActive
650	}
651	
652	func applyCandidateCycleStateToRunState(runState *RunState, state CandidateCycleState) {
653		runState.CandidateCyclePhase = state.Phase
654		runState.CandidateRef = state.CandidateRef
655		runState.CandidateRev = state.CandidateRev
656		runState.CycleIndex = state.CycleIndex
657		runState.ReviewActive = state.ReviewActive
658		runState.RepairActive = state.RepairActive
659	}

21	// PostMergeReviewInput bundles the data RunPostMergeReview needs from its
22	// caller — historically the execute-bead loop body, now the per-attempt
23	// pipeline owned by try.Attempt (ddx-a921ff01 C3). The fields mirror what
24	// the inlined block read from the worker, the candidate bead, the report,
25	// and the loop runtime.
26	type PostMergeReviewInput struct {

40	// PostMergeReviewOutput carries the review state machine's decision back to
41	// the caller. Report is the (possibly updated) ExecuteBeadReport; Approved
42	// reports whether the caller should treat this attempt as a success. When
43	// the review path encountered a non-fatal Store error the caller is expected
44	// to route StoreErr through its outcome-error handler
45	// (handleOutcomeStoreError on ExecuteBeadWorker), keyed by StoreErrOp.

48	// gap and exactly one repair cycle has been scheduled for this result_rev.
49	// The caller should run a repair attempt with MinPower above
50	// RepairContext.ImplementerActualPower while preserving all explicit
51	// harness/model/provider/profile pins from the original attempt.
52	type PostMergeReviewOutput struct {
53		Report        ExecuteBeadReport
54		Approved      bool
55		StoreErrOp    string
56		StoreErr      error
57		RepairContext *RepairContextFromReviewGroup
58	}

60	// RunPostMergeReview executes the post-merge review state machine for a
61	// successful bead attempt. When review is skipped (no reviewer or --no-review)
62	// the bead is closed and the function returns Approved=true. When review is
63	// active, the execute-loop asks the review coordinator to evaluate the
64	// close-eligible result before CloseWithEvidence runs; close only proceeds on
65	// unanimous evidence-backed APPROVE. REQUEST_CHANGES and BLOCK record their
66	// review events without reopening the bead.
67	//
68	// The review:skip label only skips when it carries a sibling
69	// review:skip-reason:* label; otherwise it is ignored. When review is not
70	// skipped, the review coordinator's verdict(s) or error drive the canonical
71	// event emissions (review / review-error / review-manual-required /
72	// review-malfunction), and on a non-APPROVE verdict the triage policy is
73	// still consulted without reopening the bead.
74	//
75	// Returns Approved=true on the APPROVE path and on the skip path; returns
76	// Approved=false on REQUEST_CHANGES, BLOCK, malformed APPROVE/BLOCK, or
77	// reviewer transport error. Store errors are surfaced via StoreErrOp +
78	// StoreErr so callers can keep the existing handleOutcomeStoreError pattern
79	// unchanged.

81	// This is the C3 (ddx-a921ff01) extraction of the 180-line review block
82	// previously inline in execute_bead_loop.go; the loop now sees a structured
83	// outcome and conflict-recovery / decomposition / no_changes paths remain
84	// in the loop body until subsequent C-series beads (C4, C5) move them too.
85	func RunPostMergeReview(ctx context.Context, in PostMergeReviewInput) PostMergeReviewOutput {
86		report := in.Report
87		out := PostMergeReviewOutput{Report: report, Approved: true}
88	
89		now := in.Now
90		if now == nil {
91			now = time.Now
92		}
93	
94		reviewSkipped := in.Reviewer == nil || in.NoReview
95		if !reviewSkipped && HasBeadLabel(in.Bead.Labels, "review:skip") {
96			reviewSkipped = HasBeadLabelPrefix(in.Bead.Labels, "review:skip-reason:")
97		}
98	
99		if reviewSkipped {
100			if err := in.Store.CloseWithEvidence(in.Bead.ID, report.SessionID, report.ResultRev); err != nil {
101				out.StoreErrOp = "CloseWithEvidence"
102				out.StoreErr = err
103				return out
104			}
105			out.Report = report
106			return out
107		}
108	
109		implRouting := ImplementerRouting{
110			Harness:     report.Harness,
111			Provider:    report.Provider,
112			Model:       report.Model,
113			ActualPower: report.ActualPower,
114			Correlation: map[string]string{
115				"bead_id":    in.Bead.ID,
116				"attempt_id": report.AttemptID,
117				"session_id": report.SessionID,
118				"result_rev": report.ResultRev,
119			},
120		}
121		reviewGroup, reviewErr := runPreCloseReviewGroup(ctx, in.Reviewer, in.Bead.ID, report.ResultRev, implRouting)
122		reviewRes, reviewGroupErr := reducePreCloseReviewGroup(reviewGroup)
123		chargeReviewCost := func() bool {
124			budgetExceeded := false
125			if capTracker := in.ReviewCostCap; capTracker != nil && reviewGroup != nil {
126				deferred := false
127				for _, slot := range reviewGroup.Slots {
128					if slot.Result == nil {
129						continue
130					}
131					capTracker.Add(slot.Result.ReviewerHarness, slot.Result.CostUSD)
132					if detail, capped := capTracker.Tripped(); capped && !deferred {
133						_ = in.Store.AppendEvent(in.Bead.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
134							Kind:      "review-cost-deferred",
135							Summary:   "review-cost-deferred",
136							Body:      ReviewCostDeferredEventBody(report.ResultRev, slot.Result.CostUSD, capTracker.Spent(), capTracker.MaxUSD),
137							Actor:     in.Assignee,
138							Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
139							CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
140						})
141						if in.Log != nil {
142							_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(in.Log, "review cost cap deferred (%s %s): %s\n", in.Bead.ID, report.ResultRev, detail)
143						}
144						deferred = true
145						budgetExceeded = true
146					}
147				}
148			}
149			return budgetExceeded

3	// execute_bead_land.go — the land coordinator pattern.
4	//
5	// This file implements the human-PR landing model for execute-bead results.
6	// The old Merge() path in execute_bead_orchestrator.go has been deleted; all
7	// target-ref writes now flow through Land(), which is called exactly once per
8	// submission by a per-project coordinator goroutine (see
9	// internal/server/workers.go:LandCoordinator).
10	//
11	// The flow mirrors how a human merges PRs:
12	//
13	//   1. Fetch the current target tip (from origin when a remote exists, or
14	//      from the local branch otherwise).
15	//   2. If the current tip equals the worker's BaseRev — fast-forward the
16	//      target branch directly to the worker's ResultRev via update-ref. The
17	//      worker's commit keeps its original parent; no new commit is created.
18	//   3. Otherwise — the target has advanced since the worker started. Create
19	//      a temporary detached worktree at the current target tip, run
20	//      `git merge --no-ff` to introduce the worker's ResultRev, and
21	//      fast-forward the target branch to the resulting merge commit. The
22	//      worker's commit is NEVER rewritten: its parent is still BaseRev, so
23	//      a later replay observes the same inputs the worker saw.
24	//   4. If the merge conflicts — abort cleanly, preserve the original
25	//      ResultRev under refs/ddx/iterations/<bead-id>/<attempt-id>-<short-tip>,
26	//      and return preserved status. Target branch is never modified.
27	//   5. If an origin remote exists — push the new target tip. The push is
28	//      strictly fast-forward; push failures are reported via PushFailed but
29	//      do not roll back the local target ref.
30	//
31	// Replay fidelity is the reason for merge-over-rebase: a rebased commit has
32	// a rewritten parent that lies about what the worker saw at execution time.
33	// A merge commit preserves both histories — the worker's original commit
34	// (parent = BaseRev) and the target's prior tip — losslessly.
35	//
36	// The in-process coordinator serializes submissions inside one process. Land()
37	// also takes the process-shared main-git lock so separate `ddx work` processes
38	// cannot interleave target-ref updates, evidence commits, or checkout sync.

143		// but the subsequent push to origin was rejected (e.g. non-fast-forward).
144		// The local state is authoritative; the remote will need to be
145		// reconciled by a later land or an operator.
146		PushFailed bool
147	
148		// PushError captures the underlying push error when PushFailed is true.
149		PushError string
150	
151		// PushConflict is true when the initial push was rejected (e.g. origin
152		// advanced) and the loop's automatic pull/merge/retry recovery ran into
153		// a merge conflict it cannot resolve without operator input. Distinct
154		// from PushFailed so the loop can park the bead for human review under
155		// a short cooldown instead of treating it as a generic push failure.
156		PushConflict bool
157	
158		// PushRecovered is true when the initial push was rejected but the loop
159		// successfully fetched, merged, and re-pushed without operator input.
160		// The land is fully complete; PushFailed and PushConflict are false.
161		// Set so callers can record telemetry on how often the auto-recovery
162		// path actually saves an operator round-trip.
163		PushRecovered bool
164	
165		// MergedCommitCount is the number of commits reachable from ResultRev but
166		// not from BaseRev — i.e. the "size" of the worker's contribution being
167		// merged in. Zero on the no-changes path. Set on both the fast-forward
168		// and merge-commit paths so metrics can compare contribution sizes.
169		MergedCommitCount int
170	
171		// EvidenceCommitSHA is set when a trailing execution-evidence commit was
172		// created after the main land. When set, NewTip points at this commit
173		// (not at the original agent commit or merge commit).
174		EvidenceCommitSHA string
175	
176		// CheckoutSyncDeferred is true when the target ref advanced but DDx left
177		// the operator checkout files untouched because dirty local paths overlap
178		// files changed by the new target tip.
179		CheckoutSyncDeferred bool
180	
181		// CheckoutSyncDeferredPaths lists the dirty paths that caused checkout sync
182		// to be skipped.
183		CheckoutSyncDeferredPaths []string
184	}

186	// PreClaimResult is the outcome of a FetchOriginAncestryCheck call.
187	type PreClaimResult struct {
188		// Action is one of:
189		//   "unchanged"     — local tip == origin tip, no action taken
190		//   "fast-forwarded"— local was behind origin; local branch was advanced
191		//   "no-origin"     — no origin remote; check skipped
192		//   "local-ahead"   — local is ahead of origin; no action needed
193		//   "diverged"      — neither is ancestor of the other; claim should be aborted
194		Action    string
195		LocalSHA  string
196		OriginSHA string
197	}

199	type fetchOriginError struct {
200		targetBranch string
201		output       string
202		err          error
203	}
204	
205	func (e *fetchOriginError) Error() string {
206		if e.output == "" {
207			return fmt.Sprintf("git fetch origin %s: %v", e.targetBranch, e.err)
208		}
209		return fmt.Sprintf("git fetch origin %s: %s: %v", e.targetBranch, e.output, e.err)
210	}
211	
212	func (e *fetchOriginError) Unwrap() error { return e.err }
213	
214	// IsIgnorableFetchOriginError reports whether a pre-claim ancestry-check
215	// failure came from the best-effort network fetch. Local worktree safety
216	// failures must propagate so workers do not claim work from an unsafe trunk.
217	func IsIgnorableFetchOriginError(err error) bool {
218		if err == nil {
219			return false
220		}
221		var fetchErr *fetchOriginError
222		if errors.As(err, &fetchErr) {
223			return true
224		}
225		msg := err.Error()
226		return strings.HasPrefix(msg, "git fetch origin ") || strings.HasPrefix(msg, "git fetch origin:")
227	}

229	// LandingGitOps abstracts the git operations Land() needs. RealLandingGitOps
230	// shells out to git; tests inject fakes or run against real temp repos.
231	type LandingGitOps interface {
232		// HasRemote reports whether the given remote name exists in dir.
233		HasRemote(dir, remote string) bool
234	
235		// CurrentBranch returns the branch HEAD currently points at in dir, or
236		// an error if HEAD is detached or unresolvable.
237		CurrentBranch(dir string) (string, error)
238	
239		// FetchBranch fetches remote/branch into dir (no merge, no checkout).
240		// Returns nil when no remote exists.
241		FetchBranch(dir, remote, branch string) error
242	
243		// ResolveRef resolves ref (e.g. "refs/heads/main" or "origin/main") to a
244		// commit SHA. When ref is unresolvable returns ("", error).
245		ResolveRef(dir, ref string) (string, error)
246	
247		// UpdateRefTo updates ref in dir to sha. When oldSHA is non-empty, the
248		// update is conditional on the current ref value equalling oldSHA.
249		UpdateRefTo(dir, ref, sha, oldSHA string) error
250	
251		// SyncWorkTreeToHead syncs the index AND the working-tree files in dir
252		// to HEAD after a non-checkout ref update (e.g. update-ref). fromRev is
253		// the commit HEAD pointed at BEFORE the ref update; it is used to
254		// compute the minimal set of tracked files changed by the update so
255		// that unrelated local modifications (agent-logs, beads.jsonl heartbeat
256		// writes, operator scratch) are NOT clobbered.
257		//
258		// Needed because Land() advances the target ref via update-ref, which
259		// touches neither the index nor the worktree. Before this fix, Land()
260		// only ran `git read-tree HEAD` to sync the index — leaving the main
261		// worktree showing phantom deleted/modified entries for every file the
262		// landed commit touched, and subsequent agents reading files from disk
263		// would see stale content.
264		//
265		// Implementation: `git read-tree HEAD` + `git diff --name-only fromRev
266		// HEAD` to enumerate changed files + `git checkout-index -f --` to
267		// materialize them from the freshly-synced index.
268		SyncWorkTreeToHead(dir, fromRev string) error
269	
270		// AddWorktree creates a new worktree at path checked out at rev in dir.
271		AddWorktree(dir, path, rev string) error
272	
273		// AddBranchWorktree creates a new worktree at path checked out on branch
274		// in dir. It is used for clean landing finalization when the operator
275		// checkout already has the target branch checked out.
276		AddBranchWorktree(dir, path, branch string) error
277	
278		// RemoveWorktree removes the worktree at path in dir (force).
279		RemoveWorktree(dir, path string) error
280	
281		// MergeInto runs `git merge --no-ff -m msg srcRev` inside wtDir, which
282		// must already be checked out at the current target tip. A clean merge
283		// produces a merge commit whose parents are [currentTip, srcRev]; the
284		// commit SHA can be read with HeadRevAt. Returns nil on clean merge,
285		// or an error on conflict. On error, the implementation is responsible
286		// for running `git merge --abort` so the worktree is left clean.
287		MergeInto(wtDir, srcRev, msg string) error
288	
289		// HeadRevAt returns HEAD of the git workdir at dir.
290		HeadRevAt(dir string) (string, error)
291	
292		// PushFFOnly pushes localRef to remote as targetBranch with strict
293		// fast-forward semantics. Returns an error when the push would not be
294		// fast-forward or when the network call fails.
295		PushFFOnly(dir, remote, localRef, targetBranch string) error
296	
297		// FetchOriginAncestryCheck fetches origin/targetBranch and compares it
298		// to the local branch tip. When origin is ahead the local branch is
299		// fast-forwarded via update-ref + read-tree. When the two tips have
300		// diverged (neither is ancestor of the other) the returned result has
301		// Action == "diverged". When no origin remote exists the result has
302		// Action == "no-origin". The caller decides whether to abort a claim.
303		FetchOriginAncestryCheck(dir, targetBranch string) (PreClaimResult, error)
304	
305		// CountCommits returns the number of commits reachable from tip but not
306		// from base (i.e. git rev-list --count base..tip). Used to record the
307		// contribution size in land metrics. Returns 0 on error.
308		CountCommits(dir, base, tip string) int
309	
310		// StageDir stages all files under relPath in dir for the next commit.
311		StageDir(dir, relPath string) error
312	
313		// CommitStaged creates a commit from currently staged changes using msg
314		// as the commit message. Returns (sha, nil) when a commit was made,
315		// ("", nil) when nothing was staged, and ("", err) on failure.
316		CommitStaged(dir, msg string) (string, error)
317	
318		// DiffNumstat returns the output of `git diff --numstat base tip --` in
319		// dir. Used by the large-deletion gate. Returning ("", nil) indicates no
320		// diffable output (e.g. in a test stub with no real repo).
321		DiffNumstat(dir, base, tip string) (string, error)
322	
323		// DiffNameOnly returns the list of changed file paths between base and tip
324		// in dir (`git diff --name-only base tip --`). Used by the syntax-sanity
325		// gate. Returning (nil, nil) indicates no changes.
326		DiffNameOnly(dir, base, tip string) ([]string, error)
327	}

384	func (RealLandingGitOps) SyncWorkTreeToHead(dir, fromRev string) error {

388	// readTreeHeadWithRetry runs `git read-tree HEAD` in dir, retrying with
389	// exponential backoff when the git index lock is held by a concurrent process.
390	//
391	// Background (ddx-7e659c95): git read-tree needs to acquire .git/index.lock.
392	// When an operator git command (git status, git commit, etc.) holds the lock
393	// concurrently, git exits 128 with "Unable to create index.lock: File exists".
394	// Without retry the main worktree index stays dirty after a merge landing,
395	// causing a subsequent `git commit beads.jsonl` to sweep in phantom reverts of
396	// the bead's changes.
397	//
398	// The retry budget (30 s, 100 ms → 1 s backoff) matches the
399	// DefaultLockRetryPolicy used by withTrackerLock so contention handling is
400	// consistent across all ddx git-index operations.
401	func readTreeHeadWithRetry(dir string) ([]byte, error) {
402		const (
403			initialBackoff = 100 * time.Millisecond
404			maxBackoff     = 1 * time.Second
405			maxElapsed     = 30 * time.Second
406		)
407		start := time.Now()
408		backoff := initialBackoff
409		for {
410			out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "read-tree", "HEAD").CombinedOutput()
411			if err == nil {
412				return out, nil
413			}
414			// Only retry on index lock contention; all other errors are fatal.
415			if !strings.Contains(string(out), "index.lock") {
416				return out, err
417			}
418			if time.Since(start) >= maxElapsed {
419				return out, err
420			}
421			time.Sleep(backoff)
422			backoff *= 2
423			if backoff > maxBackoff {
424				backoff = maxBackoff
425			}
426		}
427	}
428	
429	func syncWorkTreeToHeadExcludingPaths(dir, fromRev string, skipPaths []string) error {
430		// Step 1: sync the index to HEAD. This is required before checkout-index
431		// below will do anything useful, and also keeps subsequent CommitTracker
432		// calls from building stale trees.
433		//
434		// ddx-7e659c95: git read-tree requires .git/index.lock. If an operator
435		// git command (git status, git commit, etc.) holds the lock concurrently,
436		// git read-tree exits 128 with "Unable to create index.lock: File exists".
437		// Retry with exponential backoff for up to 30 s so transient operator
438		// contention does not leave the main worktree index dirty.
439		out, err := readTreeHeadWithRetry(dir)
440		if err != nil {
441			return fmt.Errorf("git read-tree HEAD: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
442		}
443	
444		// Step 2: compute the list of tracked files changed between the previous
445		// HEAD and the current HEAD. These are the files that the landing commit
446		// added, modified, or deleted. We only restore THESE files to avoid
447		// clobbering unrelated local modifications (agent-logs being written by
448		// the running server, beads.jsonl heartbeat updates, operator scratch).
449		if fromRev == "" {
450			// No previous HEAD known — fall back to the unsafe behaviour of
451			// read-tree only. Acceptable when the caller is a best-effort path
452			// that cannot reconstruct fromRev.
453			return nil
454		}
455		diffOut, diffErr := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "diff", "--name-only", fromRev, "HEAD").CombinedOutput()
456		if diffErr != nil {
457			// Diff failed (bad fromRev, shallow history, etc.) — leave the
458			// worktree stale rather than risk a broken checkout. The CommitTracker
459			// stale-tree bug is the prior status quo and no worse than before.
460			return nil
461		}
462		changed := strings.Fields(strings.TrimSpace(string(diffOut)))
463		if len(changed) == 0 {
464			return nil
465		}
466	
467		// Step 3: split into existing-in-HEAD (checkout-index) and
468		// deleted-in-HEAD (os.Remove) buckets. checkout-index only writes files
469		// that are in the index; it cannot represent a deletion, so we handle
470		// those ourselves.
471		skip := map[string]bool{}
472		for _, path := range skipPaths {
473			skip[filepath.ToSlash(path)] = true
474		}
475		var indexFiles []string
476		var removedFiles []string
477		for _, f := range changed {
478			if skip[filepath.ToSlash(f)] {
479				continue
480			}
481			probe := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "ls-files", "--error-unmatch", "--", f)
482			if probe.Run() == nil {
483				indexFiles = append(indexFiles, f)
484			} else {
485				removedFiles = append(removedFiles, f)
486			}
487		}
488	
489		// Step 4: materialize the index-present files into the working tree.
490		// -f overwrites any stale content at these exact paths. Unrelated files
491		// are untouched because we pass the specific path list.
492		if len(indexFiles) > 0 {
493			for _, f := range indexFiles {
494				if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(filepath.Join(dir, f)), 0o755); err != nil {
495					return fmt.Errorf("creating checkout parent for %s: %w", f, err)
496				}
497			}
498			args := []string{"checkout-index", "-f", "--"}
499			args = append(args, indexFiles...)
500			out2, err2 := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, args...).CombinedOutput()
501			if err2 != nil {
502				return fmt.Errorf("git checkout-index -f: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out2)), err2)
503			}
504		}
505	
506		// Step 5: remove files that the landing commit deleted and whose removal
507		// did not propagate to the worktree because update-ref bypassed the
508		// working-tree update.
509		for _, f := range removedFiles {
510			full := filepath.Join(dir, f)
511			_ = os.Remove(full) // best-effort; leave the file if removal fails
512		}
513	
514		return nil
515	}

517	func syncWorkTreeToHeadGuarded(gitOps LandingGitOps, dir, fromRev string, dirtyBefore []string, result *LandResult) {
518		overlap, err := checkoutSyncDirtyOverlapPaths(dir, fromRev, dirtyBefore)
519		if err == nil && len(overlap) > 0 {
520			if result != nil {
521				result.CheckoutSyncDeferred = true
522				result.CheckoutSyncDeferredPaths = appendUniqueStrings(result.CheckoutSyncDeferredPaths, overlap...)
523			}
524			_ = syncWorkTreeToHeadExcludingPaths(dir, fromRev, overlap)
525			return
526		}
527		_ = gitOps.SyncWorkTreeToHead(dir, fromRev)
528	}

530	func ensureLandingWorktreeReady(dir, targetBranch string) error {
531		if !isInsideGitWorktree(dir) {
532			return nil
533		}
534		branchOut, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "branch", "--show-current").CombinedOutput()
535		if err != nil {
536			return fmt.Errorf("checking landing branch: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(branchOut)), err)
537		}
538		branch := strings.TrimSpace(string(branchOut))
539		if branch == "" {
540			return fmt.Errorf("landing worktree is detached; expected branch %q", targetBranch)
541		}
542		if targetBranch != "" && branch != targetBranch {
543			return fmt.Errorf("landing branch mismatch: on %q, want %q", branch, targetBranch)
544		}
545		return waitForEmptyGitIndex(dir, 2*time.Second)
546	}

548	func isInsideGitWorktree(dir string) bool {
549		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "rev-parse", "--is-inside-work-tree").CombinedOutput()
550		return err == nil && strings.TrimSpace(string(out)) == "true"
551	}

553	func waitForEmptyGitIndex(dir string, timeout time.Duration) error {
554		deadline := time.Now().Add(timeout)
555		for {
556			if err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "diff", "--cached", "--quiet").Run(); err == nil {
557				return nil
558			}
559			if time.Now().After(deadline) {
560				stagedOut, _ := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "diff", "--cached", "--name-status").CombinedOutput()
561				staged := strings.TrimSpace(string(stagedOut))
562				if staged == "" {
563					staged = "unknown staged changes"
564				}
565				return fmt.Errorf("landing worktree has staged changes after waiting %s:\n%s", timeout, staged)
566			}
567			time.Sleep(100 * time.Millisecond)
568		}
569	}

571	func checkoutSyncDirtyOverlapPaths(dir, fromRev string, dirtyPaths []string) ([]string, error) {
572		if fromRev == "" || len(dirtyPaths) == 0 {
573			return nil, nil
574		}
575		diffOut, diffErr := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "diff", "--name-only", fromRev, "HEAD").CombinedOutput()
576		if diffErr != nil {
577			return nil, diffErr
578		}
579		changed := map[string]bool{}
580		for _, path := range strings.Fields(strings.TrimSpace(string(diffOut))) {
581			changed[filepath.ToSlash(path)] = true
582		}
583		if len(changed) == 0 {
584			return nil, nil
585		}
586		var overlap []string
587		for _, path := range dirtyPaths {
588			slashPath := filepath.ToSlash(path)
589			if checkoutSyncDeferralIgnoredPath(slashPath) {
590				continue
591			}
592			if changed[slashPath] {
593				overlap = append(overlap, slashPath)
594			}
595		}
596		return overlap, nil
597	}
598	
599	func checkoutSyncDeferralIgnoredPath(path string) bool {
600		if strings.HasPrefix(path, ".ddx/executions/") ||
601			strings.HasPrefix(path, ".ddx/runs/") ||
602			strings.HasPrefix(path, ".ddx/backups/") ||
603			strings.HasPrefix(path, ".ddx/run-state/") ||
604			strings.HasPrefix(path, ".ddx/.git-tracker.lock/") {
605			return true
606		}
607		return path == ".ddx/run-state.json" || path == ExecutionCleanupMetadataFileName
608	}

610	func appendUniqueStrings(existing []string, additions ...string) []string {
611		seen := make(map[string]bool, len(existing)+len(additions))
612		for _, value := range existing {
613			seen[value] = true
614		}
615		for _, value := range additions {
616			if seen[value] {
617				continue
618			}
619			seen[value] = true
620			existing = append(existing, value)
621		}
622		return existing
623	}

648	func (RealLandingGitOps) MergeInto(wtDir, srcRev, msg string) error {
649		// --no-ff forces a merge commit even when the merge could fast-forward
650		// (which shouldn't happen given our caller's ancestry check, but is a
651		// defensive guarantee that target history always gets a marker commit).
652		// We inject user.name/user.email via -c so the merge commit can be
653		// created even when the worktree inherited no git config; the
654		// coordinator is a machine actor and should not adopt a human's identity.
655		out, err := internalgit.Command(
656			context.Background(), wtDir,
657			"-c", "user.name=ddx-land-coordinator",
658			"-c", "user.email=coordinator@ddx.local",
659			"merge", "--no-ff", "-m", msg, srcRev,
660		).CombinedOutput()
661		if err != nil {
662			_ = internalgit.Command(context.Background(), wtDir, "merge", "--abort").Run()
663			return fmt.Errorf("git merge --no-ff %s: %s: %w", srcRev, strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
664		}
665		return nil
666	}

676	func (RealLandingGitOps) PushFFOnly(dir, remote, localRef, targetBranch string) error {
677		// Refspec "<local>:<remote>" with no '+' prefix → fast-forward only.
678		refspec := fmt.Sprintf("%s:refs/heads/%s", localRef, targetBranch)
679		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "push", remote, refspec).CombinedOutput()
680		if err != nil {
681			return fmt.Errorf("git push %s %s: %s: %w", remote, refspec, strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)

695				n = n*10 + int(c-'0')

697		}

770	
771	func fetchOriginAncestryCheckLocked(dir, targetBranch string) (PreClaimResult, error) {
772		if err := ensureLandingWorktreeReady(dir, targetBranch); err != nil {
773			return PreClaimResult{}, err
774		}
775	
776		// Step 1: check for origin remote.
777		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "remote", "get-url", "origin").CombinedOutput()
778		if err != nil {
779			// No origin remote — single-machine case; skip check.
780			return PreClaimResult{Action: "no-origin"}, nil
781		}
782		_ = out // remote URL not needed
783	
784		// Step 2: fetch origin/targetBranch (best-effort; failure is non-fatal but surfaced).
785		if fetchOut, fetchErr := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "fetch", "origin", targetBranch).CombinedOutput(); fetchErr != nil {
786			return PreClaimResult{}, &fetchOriginError{
787				targetBranch: targetBranch,
788				output:       strings.TrimSpace(string(fetchOut)),
789				err:          fetchErr,
790			}
791		}
792	
793		// Step 3: resolve local tip.
794		localOut, localErr := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "rev-parse", "--verify", "refs/heads/"+targetBranch).CombinedOutput()
795		if localErr != nil {
796			return PreClaimResult{}, fmt.Errorf("resolving local %s: %s: %w", targetBranch, strings.TrimSpace(string(localOut)), localErr)
797		}
798		localSHA := strings.TrimSpace(string(localOut))
799	
800		// Step 4: resolve origin tip.
801		originOut, originErr := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "rev-parse", "--verify", "refs/remotes/origin/"+targetBranch).CombinedOutput()
802		if originErr != nil {
803			return PreClaimResult{}, fmt.Errorf("resolving origin/%s: %s: %w", targetBranch, strings.TrimSpace(string(originOut)), originErr)
804		}
805		originSHA := strings.TrimSpace(string(originOut))
806	
807		// Step 5: compare.
808		if localSHA == originSHA {
809			return PreClaimResult{Action: "unchanged", LocalSHA: localSHA, OriginSHA: originSHA}, nil
810		}
811	
812		// Is local an ancestor of origin? (origin is ahead)
813		localAncestorErr := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "merge-base", "--is-ancestor", localSHA, originSHA).Run()
814		if localAncestorErr == nil {
815			// Origin is ahead: fast-forward local branch via update-ref + sync worktree.
816			targetRef := "refs/heads/" + targetBranch
817			if upErr := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "update-ref", targetRef, originSHA, localSHA).Run(); upErr != nil {
818				return PreClaimResult{}, fmt.Errorf("fast-forwarding %s to %s: %w", targetRef, originSHA, upErr)
819			}
820			// Sync index + working tree to new HEAD so the main worktree files
821			// reflect the pulled-down origin commits. Pass localSHA as fromRev
822			// to restrict the restore to files actually changed by origin's
823			// advance, preserving unrelated local modifications.
824			_ = (RealLandingGitOps{}).SyncWorkTreeToHead(dir, localSHA)
825			return PreClaimResult{Action: "fast-forwarded", LocalSHA: localSHA, OriginSHA: originSHA}, nil
826		}
827	
828		// Is origin an ancestor of local? (local is ahead)
829		originAncestorErr := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "merge-base", "--is-ancestor", originSHA, localSHA).Run()
830		if originAncestorErr == nil {
831			return PreClaimResult{Action: "local-ahead", LocalSHA: localSHA, OriginSHA: originSHA}, nil
832		}
833	
834		// Neither is ancestor of the other: diverged.
835		return PreClaimResult{Action: "diverged", LocalSHA: localSHA, OriginSHA: originSHA}, nil
836	}

838	// landIterationRef returns the documented hidden ref for a land-time preserve.
839	// Format: refs/ddx/iterations/<bead-id>/<attempt-id>-<short-tip>. The short-tip
840	// captures the current target tip at the time of the conflict so subsequent
841	// recovery tools can reconstruct which merge target was in play.
842	func landIterationRef(beadID, attemptID, tip string) string {
843		short := tip
844		if len(short) > 12 {
845			short = short[:12]
846		}
847		attempt := attemptID
848		if attempt == "" {
849			attempt = NowFunc().UTC().Format("20060102T150405Z")
850		}
851		return fmt.Sprintf("refs/ddx/iterations/%s/%s-%s", beadID, attempt, short)
852	}

854	// landEvidence creates a trailing commit that folds the per-attempt execution
855	// evidence directory into the target branch. Called after the main land (ff or
856	// merge) succeeds and before the push. The evidence commit is a normal child of
857	// result.NewTip — the agent's original commit SHA is not amended.
858	func landEvidence(wd, targetBranch string, req LandRequest, gitOps LandingGitOps, result *LandResult) error {
859		branch, err := gitOps.CurrentBranch(wd)
860		if err != nil {
861			return fmt.Errorf("evidence commit branch check: %w", err)
862		}
863		if branch != targetBranch {
864			return fmt.Errorf("evidence commit branch mismatch: on %q, want %q", branch, targetBranch)
865		}
866		if err := gitOps.StageDir(wd, req.EvidenceDir); err != nil {
867			return fmt.Errorf("stage evidence: %w", err)
868		}
869		msg := fmt.Sprintf("chore: add execution evidence [%s]", shortAttempt(req.AttemptID))
870		sha, err := gitOps.CommitStaged(wd, msg)
871		if err != nil {
872			return fmt.Errorf("commit evidence: %w", err)
873		}
874		if sha == "" {
875			// Evidence already committed in the working tree (worktree-origin path):
876			// the bundle was committed inside the attempt worktree as part of ResultRev
877			// so it is present at HEAD when the landing finalization worktree is checked
878			// out. No trailing commit needed; verify tracked files exist then accept.
879			if !evidenceDirHasTrackedFiles(wd, req.EvidenceDir) {
880				return fmt.Errorf("commit evidence: no staged evidence files under %s", req.EvidenceDir)
881			}
882			headSHA, headErr := gitOps.HeadRevAt(wd)
883			if headErr != nil {
884				return fmt.Errorf("evidence already committed, reading HEAD: %w", headErr)
885			}
886			result.EvidenceCommitSHA = headSHA
887			return nil
888		}
889		result.EvidenceCommitSHA = sha
890		result.NewTip = sha
891		return nil
892	}

894	// evidenceDirHasTrackedFiles reports whether any files under dirRel are tracked
895	// in git at wd. Used by landEvidence to distinguish "nothing staged because
896	// already committed" from "nothing staged because evidence is absent."
897	func evidenceDirHasTrackedFiles(wd, dirRel string) bool {
898		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), wd, "ls-files", "--", filepath.FromSlash(dirRel)).Output()
899		return err == nil && len(strings.TrimSpace(string(out))) > 0
900	}

922	func copyEvidenceDirForLanding(projectRoot, landingWD, relPath string) error {
923		if relPath == "" || sameFilesystemPath(projectRoot, landingWD) {
924			return nil
925		}
926		cleanRel, ok := cleanRelativePath(relPath)
927		if !ok {
928			return fmt.Errorf("invalid evidence path %q", relPath)
929		}
930		if evidenceDirHasTrackedFiles(landingWD, cleanRel) {
931			return nil
932		}
933		src := filepath.Join(projectRoot, cleanRel)
934		dst := filepath.Join(landingWD, cleanRel)
935		info, err := os.Stat(src)
936		if err != nil {
937			if os.IsNotExist(err) {
938				return nil
939			}
940			return err
941		}
942		if !info.IsDir() {
943			return nil
944		}
945		if err := os.RemoveAll(dst); err != nil {
946			return err
947		}
948		return filepath.WalkDir(src, func(path string, d os.DirEntry, err error) error {
949			if err != nil {
950				return err
951			}
952			rel, err := filepath.Rel(src, path)
953			if err != nil {
954				return err
955			}
956			target := filepath.Join(dst, rel)
957			if d.IsDir() {
958				return os.MkdirAll(target, 0o755)
959			}
960			info, err := d.Info()
961			if err != nil {
962				return err
963			}
964			data, err := os.ReadFile(path)
965			if err != nil {
966				return err
967			}
968			return os.WriteFile(target, data, info.Mode().Perm())
969		})
970	}

995	func cleanRelativePath(path string) (string, bool) {
996		if path == "" || filepath.IsAbs(path) {
997			return "", false
998		}
999		clean := filepath.Clean(filepath.FromSlash(path))
1000		if clean == "." || strings.HasPrefix(clean, ".."+string(os.PathSeparator)) || clean == ".." {

1002		}
1003		return clean, true

1006	func sameFilesystemPath(a, b string) bool {
1007		if a == "" || b == "" {
1008			return false
1009		}
1010		absA, err := filepath.Abs(a)
1011		if err != nil {
1012			absA = filepath.Clean(a)
1013		}
1014		absB, err := filepath.Abs(b)
1015		if err != nil {
1016			absB = filepath.Clean(b)
1017		}
1018		if resolvedA, err := filepath.EvalSymlinks(absA); err == nil {
1019			absA = resolvedA
1020		}
1021		if resolvedB, err := filepath.EvalSymlinks(absB); err == nil {
1022			absB = resolvedB
1023		}
1024		return absA == absB
1025	}

1027	// Land performs fetch → (ff or merge) → push for a single submission.
1028	// It serializes primary-checkout git operations with the process-shared
1029	// main-git lock so separate ddx work processes cannot interleave landing with
1030	// tracker commits or pre-dispatch checkpoint/ref updates.
1031	//
1032	// projectRoot is the directory containing the project's .git. req.WorktreeDir,
1033	// when non-empty, is used as the git working directory for all commands; when
1034	// empty, projectRoot is used. Both usually refer to the same dir — the field
1035	// is kept for forward compatibility with multi-clone topologies.
1036	func Land(projectRoot string, req LandRequest, gitOps LandingGitOps) (*LandResult, error) {
1037		var result *LandResult
1038		err := withMainGitLock(projectRoot, func() error {
1039			var landErr error
1040			result, landErr = landLocked(projectRoot, req, gitOps)
1041			return landErr
1042		})
1043		return result, err
1044	}
1045	
1046	func landLocked(projectRoot string, req LandRequest, gitOps LandingGitOps) (*LandResult, error) {
1047		if gitOps == nil {
1048			gitOps = RealLandingGitOps{}
1049		}
1050		wd := req.WorktreeDir
1051		if wd == "" {
1052			wd = projectRoot
1053		}
1054	
1055		// Trivial guard — no commits to land.
1056		if req.ResultRev == "" || req.ResultRev == req.BaseRev {
1057			return &LandResult{Status: "no-changes", Reason: "result_rev == base_rev"}, nil
1058		}
1059		syncOperatorAfterLand := sameFilesystemPath(projectRoot, wd)
1060		var operatorDirtyBeforeLand []string
1061		if syncOperatorAfterLand {
1062			operatorDirtyBeforeLand = dirtyWorktreePaths(wd)
1063		}
1064	
1065		// Resolve target branch (default to project HEAD).
1066		targetBranch := req.TargetBranch
1067		if targetBranch == "" {
1068			br, err := gitOps.CurrentBranch(wd)
1069			if err != nil {
1070				return nil, fmt.Errorf("resolving target branch: %w", err)
1071			}
1072			targetBranch = br
1073		}
1074		if err := ensureLandingWorktreeReady(wd, targetBranch); err != nil {
1075			return nil, err
1076		}
1077		targetRef := "refs/heads/" + targetBranch
1078	
1079		// Fetch the current target tip from origin when available; otherwise
1080		// read the local branch directly.
1081		hasOrigin := gitOps.HasRemote(wd, "origin")
1082		if hasOrigin {
1083			// Fetch is best-effort: a disconnected remote must not block
1084			// a local land. Log-style errors are swallowed here; the
1085			// subsequent ResolveRef will surface any fatal issue.
1086			_ = gitOps.FetchBranch(wd, "origin", targetBranch)
1087		}
1088		currentTip, err := gitOps.ResolveRef(wd, targetRef)
1089		if err != nil {
1090			return nil, fmt.Errorf("resolving target tip %s: %w", targetRef, err)
1091		}
1092	
1093		contribCount := gitOps.CountCommits(wd, req.BaseRev, req.ResultRev)
1094	
1095		if preserved, err := preserveIfLargeDeletion(wd, req, gitOps, currentTip, contribCount); err != nil {
1096			return nil, err
1097		} else if preserved != nil {
1098			return preserved, nil
1099		}
1100		if preserved, err := preserveIfSyntaxSanityFails(wd, req, gitOps, currentTip, contribCount); err != nil {
1101			return nil, err
1102		} else if preserved != nil {
1103			return preserved, nil
1104		}
1105	
1106		// Fast path: no sibling advanced the target branch → straight ff via
1107		// update-ref. The worker's commit becomes the new tip unchanged; its
1108		// parent is still BaseRev, so replay sees the same inputs the worker
1109		// saw. No merge commit is created.
1110		if currentTip == req.BaseRev {
1111			if err := gitOps.UpdateRefTo(wd, targetRef, req.ResultRev, currentTip); err != nil {
1112				return nil, fmt.Errorf("fast-forwarding %s to %s: %w", targetRef, req.ResultRev, err)
1113			}
1114			result := &LandResult{
1115				Status:            "landed",
1116				NewTip:            req.ResultRev,
1117				Merged:            false,
1118				MergedCommitCount: contribCount,
1119			}
1120			finalWD, cleanup, err := landingFinalizationWorktree(projectRoot, wd, targetBranch, gitOps)
1121			if err != nil {
1122				return nil, err
1123			}
1124			defer cleanup()
1125			if preserved, err := preserveIfPostLandGateFails(finalWD, req, gitOps, targetRef, currentTip, result.NewTip, contribCount, nil); err != nil {
1126				return nil, err
1127			} else if preserved != nil {
1128				return preserved, nil
1129			}
1130			if req.EvidenceDir != "" {
1131				if err := copyEvidenceDirForLanding(projectRoot, finalWD, req.EvidenceDir); err != nil {
1132					return nil, fmt.Errorf("copying evidence into landing worktree: %w", err)
1133				}
1134				if err := landEvidence(finalWD, targetBranch, req, gitOps, result); err != nil {
1135					return preserveAfterEvidenceFailure(finalWD, req, gitOps, targetRef, currentTip, result.NewTip, contribCount, nil, err)
1136				}
1137				cleanupProjectEvidenceCopy(projectRoot, req.EvidenceDir)
1138			}
1139			landPush(finalWD, targetBranch, result.NewTip, hasOrigin, gitOps, result)
1140			if syncOperatorAfterLand {
1141				syncWorkTreeToHeadGuarded(gitOps, wd, currentTip, operatorDirtyBeforeLand, result)
1142			}
1143			return result, nil
1144		}
1145	
1146		// Merge path: the target has advanced since the worker started. Create
1147		// a temp detached worktree at currentTip and run `git merge --no-ff
1148		// ResultRev` inside it. The result is a merge commit whose parents are
1149		// [currentTip, ResultRev]. Crucially, ResultRev itself is NOT rewritten:
1150		// its parent is still BaseRev, so replay observes the original inputs.
1151		tempWT, tempWtErr := os.MkdirTemp("", "ddx-land-wt-*")
1152		if tempWtErr != nil {
1153			return nil, fmt.Errorf("creating temp worktree dir: %w", tempWtErr)
1154		}
1155		// os.MkdirTemp creates the dir, but git worktree add refuses to run if
1156		// the target already exists. Remove it first so git can recreate it.
1157		_ = os.RemoveAll(tempWT)
1158		if err := gitOps.AddWorktree(wd, tempWT, currentTip); err != nil {
1159			return nil, fmt.Errorf("adding temp worktree at %s: %w", currentTip, err)
1160		}
1161		defer func() { _ = gitOps.RemoveWorktree(wd, tempWT) }()
1162	
1163		mergeMsg := fmt.Sprintf("Merge bead %s attempt %s into %s", req.BeadID, shortAttempt(req.AttemptID), targetBranch)
1164		if err := gitOps.MergeInto(tempWT, req.ResultRev, mergeMsg); err != nil {
1165			// Merge conflict: preserve the original ResultRev and return.
1166			preserveRef := landIterationRef(req.BeadID, req.AttemptID, currentTip)
1167			if upErr := gitOps.UpdateRefTo(wd, preserveRef, req.ResultRev, ""); upErr != nil {
1168				return nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving %s after merge conflict: %w", preserveRef, upErr)
1169			}
1170			return &LandResult{
1171				Status:            "preserved",
1172				PreserveRef:       preserveRef,
1173				Reason:            "merge conflict",
1174				MergedCommitCount: contribCount,
1175			}, nil
1176		}
1177	
1178		// Merge clean: read the merge commit SHA from the temp worktree's HEAD
1179		// and fast-forward the target branch to it.
1180		mergeSHA, err := gitOps.HeadRevAt(tempWT)
1181		if err != nil {
1182			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reading merge HEAD: %w", err)
1183		}
1184		if err := gitOps.UpdateRefTo(wd, targetRef, mergeSHA, currentTip); err != nil {
1185			return nil, fmt.Errorf("fast-forwarding %s to merge commit %s: %w", targetRef, mergeSHA, err)
1186		}
1187		result := &LandResult{
1188			Status:            "landed",
1189			NewTip:            mergeSHA,
1190			Merged:            true,
1191			MergedCommitCount: contribCount,
1192		}
1193		finalWD, cleanup, err := landingFinalizationWorktree(projectRoot, wd, targetBranch, gitOps)
1194		if err != nil {
1195			return nil, err
1196		}
1197		defer cleanup()
1198		if preserved, err := preserveIfPostLandGateFails(finalWD, req, gitOps, targetRef, currentTip, result.NewTip, contribCount, nil); err != nil {
1199			return nil, err
1200		} else if preserved != nil {
1201			return preserved, nil
1202		}
1203		if req.EvidenceDir != "" {
1204			if err := copyEvidenceDirForLanding(projectRoot, finalWD, req.EvidenceDir); err != nil {
1205				return nil, fmt.Errorf("copying evidence into landing worktree: %w", err)
1206			}
1207			if err := landEvidence(finalWD, targetBranch, req, gitOps, result); err != nil {
1208				return preserveAfterEvidenceFailure(finalWD, req, gitOps, targetRef, currentTip, result.NewTip, contribCount, nil, err)
1209			}
1210			cleanupProjectEvidenceCopy(projectRoot, req.EvidenceDir)
1211		}
1212		landPush(finalWD, targetBranch, result.NewTip, hasOrigin, gitOps, result)
1213		if syncOperatorAfterLand {
1214			syncWorkTreeToHeadGuarded(gitOps, wd, currentTip, operatorDirtyBeforeLand, result)
1215		}
1216		return result, nil
1217	}

1219	func preserveIfPostLandGateFails(wd string, req LandRequest, gitOps LandingGitOps, targetRef, preLandTip, landedTip string, contribCount int, dirtyBefore []string) (*LandResult, error) {
1220		if len(req.PostLandCommand) == 0 {

1222		}

1224		if err == nil {

1230			return nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving %s after post-land gate: %w", preserveRef, upErr)

1233			return nil, fmt.Errorf("restoring %s to %s after post-land gate failed: %w", targetRef, preLandTip, revertErr)

1242			PreserveRef:       preserveRef,
1243			Reason:            reason,
1244			MergedCommitCount: contribCount,

1270	func runPostLandCommand(wd string, command []string) (string, error) {
1271		if len(command) == 0 {
1272			return "", nil
1273		}
1274		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 30*time.Minute)
1275		defer cancel()
1276		cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, command[0], command[1:]...)
1277		cmd.Dir = wd
1278		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
1279		if ctx.Err() == context.DeadlineExceeded {
1280			return string(out), ctx.Err()
1281		}
1282		return string(out), err

1298	func preserveIfLargeDeletion(wd string, req LandRequest, gitOps LandingGitOps, currentTip string, contribCount int) (*LandResult, error) {
1299		threshold := largeDeletionLineThreshold(req)
1300		finding, found, err := largestDeletionFinding(gitOps, wd, req.BaseRev, req.ResultRev, threshold)
1301		if err != nil {
1302			return nil, err
1303		}
1304		if !found {
1305			return nil, nil
1306		}
1307		ack, err := landHasLargeDeletionAcknowledgement(wd, req.BaseRev, req.ResultRev)
1308		if err == nil && ack {
1309			return nil, nil
1310		}
1311	
1312		preserveRef := landIterationRef(req.BeadID, req.AttemptID, currentTip)
1313		if upErr := gitOps.UpdateRefTo(wd, preserveRef, req.ResultRev, ""); upErr != nil {
1314			return nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving %s after large-deletion gate: %w", preserveRef, upErr)
1315		}
1316		return &LandResult{
1317			Status:            "preserved",
1318			PreserveRef:       preserveRef,
1319			Reason:            fmt.Sprintf("large-deletion gate: %s deleted %d lines (threshold %d) without intentional large deletion acknowledgement", finding.Path, finding.Deleted, threshold),
1320			MergedCommitCount: contribCount,
1321		}, nil
1322	}

1324	func largeDeletionLineThreshold(req LandRequest) int {
1325		if req.LargeDeletionLineThreshold > 0 {
1326			return req.LargeDeletionLineThreshold
1327		}
1328		return defaultLargeDeletionLineThreshold
1329	}

1331	func largestDeletionFinding(gitOps LandingGitOps, wd, baseRev, resultRev string, threshold int) (largeDeletionFinding, bool, error) {
1332		raw, err := gitOps.DiffNumstat(wd, baseRev, resultRev)
1333		if err != nil {
1334			return largeDeletionFinding{}, false, fmt.Errorf("checking large deletions: %w", err)
1335		}
1336		var largest largeDeletionFinding
1337		for _, line := range strings.Split(strings.TrimSpace(raw), "\n") {
1338			if strings.TrimSpace(line) == "" {
1339				continue
1340			}
1341			parts := strings.SplitN(line, "\t", 3)
1342			if len(parts) != 3 || parts[1] == "-" {
1343				continue
1344			}
1345			deleted, parseErr := strconv.Atoi(parts[1])
1346			if parseErr != nil {
1347				continue
1348			}
1349			if deleted > threshold && deleted > largest.Deleted {
1350				largest = largeDeletionFinding{Path: parts[2], Deleted: deleted}
1351			}
1352		}
1353		return largest, largest.Path != "", nil
1354	}

1356	func landHasLargeDeletionAcknowledgement(wd, baseRev, resultRev string) (bool, error) {
1357		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), wd, "log", "--format=%B", baseRev+".."+resultRev).CombinedOutput()
1358		if err != nil {
1359			return false, fmt.Errorf("checking large-deletion acknowledgement: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
1360		}
1361		msg := strings.ToLower(string(out))
1362		for _, marker := range []string{
1363			"intentional large deletion",
1364			"intentional file removal",
1365			"intentional file deletion",
1366		} {
1367			if strings.Contains(msg, marker) {
1368				return true, nil
1369			}
1370		}
1371		return false, nil
1372	}

1400	func syntaxSanityFindingForResult(gitOps LandingGitOps, wd, baseRev, resultRev string) (syntaxSanityFinding, bool, error) {
1401		paths, err := gitOps.DiffNameOnly(wd, baseRev, resultRev)
1402		if err != nil {
1403			return syntaxSanityFinding{}, false, err
1404		}
1405		for _, path := range paths {
1406			result, ok, err := gitFileAt(wd, resultRev, path)
1407			if err != nil {
1408				return syntaxSanityFinding{}, false, err
1409			}
1410			if !ok {
1411				continue // deleted files are handled by deletion gates, not syntax checks.
1412			}
1413			base, _, err := gitFileAt(wd, baseRev, path)
1414			if err != nil {
1415				return syntaxSanityFinding{}, false, err
1416			}
1417			if reason, bad := syntaxSanityFailure(path, base, result); bad {
1418				return syntaxSanityFinding{Path: path, Reason: reason}, true, nil
1419			}
1420		}
1421		return syntaxSanityFinding{}, false, nil
1422	}

1424	func gitFileAt(wd, rev, path string) ([]byte, bool, error) {
1425		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), wd, "show", rev+":"+path).CombinedOutput()
1426		if err != nil {
1427			if strings.Contains(string(out), "does not exist") || strings.Contains(string(out), "exists on disk, but not in") {
1428				return nil, false, nil
1429			}
1430			return nil, false, fmt.Errorf("reading %s at %s: %s: %w", path, rev, strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
1431		}
1432		return out, true, nil
1433	}

1435	func syntaxSanityFailure(path string, base, result []byte) (string, bool) {
1436		switch strings.ToLower(filepath.Ext(path)) {
1437		case ".json":
1438			var v any
1439			if err := json.Unmarshal(result, &v); err != nil {
1440				return "invalid JSON: " + err.Error(), true
1441			}
1442		case ".go":
1443			if _, err := parser.ParseFile(token.NewFileSet(), path, result, parser.AllErrors); err != nil {
1444				return "invalid Go syntax: " + err.Error(), true
1445			}
1446		case ".svelte":
1447			return svelteSanityFailure(base, result)
1448		}
1449		return "", false
1450	}
1451	
1452	func svelteSanityFailure(base, result []byte) (string, bool) {
1453		baseHadScript := bytes.Contains(base, []byte("<script"))
1454		resultHasScript := bytes.Contains(result, []byte("<script"))
1455		resultClosesScript := bytes.Contains(result, []byte("</script>"))
1456		if baseHadScript && (!resultHasScript || !resultClosesScript) {
1457			return "Svelte file lost script structure", true
1458		}
1459		if resultHasScript && !resultClosesScript {
1460			return "Svelte script tag is not closed", true
1461		}
1462		return "", false
1463	}

1465	// landPush pushes the new target tip to origin when a remote exists. Push
1466	// failure is non-fatal for the local land outcome; it is surfaced via
1467	// PushFailed/PushError on the result.
1468	//
1469	// On the first push rejection landPush attempts one automatic recovery —
1470	// fetch origin, merge our local tip into the new origin tip, advance the
1471	// local target ref, and retry the push. The race-with-remote case (origin
1472	// advanced with non-overlapping work between our local merge and our push)
1473	// is the common failure in a multi-worker / multi-operator setup and can be
1474	// fixed without operator intervention. If the auto-merge produces a conflict
1475	// the loop cannot resolve, landPush marks the result PushConflict (a distinct
1476	// signal from generic PushFailed) so the caller can park the bead for human
1477	// review under a short cooldown rather than the long park used for
1478	// non-recoverable push failures.
1479	func landPush(wd, targetBranch, newTip string, hasOrigin bool, gitOps LandingGitOps, result *LandResult) {
1480		if !hasOrigin {
1481			return
1482		}
1483		firstErr := gitOps.PushFFOnly(wd, "origin", newTip, targetBranch)
1484		if firstErr == nil {
1485			return
1486		}
1487		// First push rejected. Try one automatic pull/merge/retry-push pass.
1488		if recovered, mergeSHA, recErr := landPushAutoRecover(wd, targetBranch, newTip, gitOps); recovered {
1489			result.NewTip = mergeSHA
1490			result.PushRecovered = true
1491			return
1492		} else if recErr != nil && isPushAutoMergeConflict(recErr) {
1493			// Auto-merge could not be resolved without operator input.
1494			result.PushConflict = true
1495			result.PushError = recErr.Error()
1496			return
1497		}
1498		// Either recovery itself failed for non-conflict reasons (network, auth,
1499		// fetch error, retry push still rejected) or origin was already at our
1500		// tip. Surface the original push error so operator messages match the
1501		// historical contract.
1502		result.PushFailed = true
1503		result.PushError = firstErr.Error()
1504	}
1505	
1506	// landPushAutoMergeConflictError tags a recovery error that originates from
1507	// an unresolvable git merge conflict, so landPush can branch on it without
1508	// pattern-matching free-form error strings.
1509	type landPushAutoMergeConflictError struct{ inner error }
1510	
1511	func (e *landPushAutoMergeConflictError) Error() string { return e.inner.Error() }
1512	func (e *landPushAutoMergeConflictError) Unwrap() error { return e.inner }
1513	
1514	func isPushAutoMergeConflict(err error) bool {
1515		for err != nil {
1516			if _, ok := err.(*landPushAutoMergeConflictError); ok {
1517				return true
1518			}
1519			type unwrapper interface{ Unwrap() error }
1520			u, ok := err.(unwrapper)
1521			if !ok {
1522				return false
1523			}
1524			err = u.Unwrap()
1525		}
1526		return false
1527	}
1528	
1529	// landPushAutoRecover attempts one fetch + merge + retry-push pass after the
1530	// initial push was rejected. Returns recovered=true and the new merge SHA
1531	// when the retry-push succeeds; otherwise returns recovered=false plus an
1532	// error that indicates whether the recovery hit a merge conflict (wrapped in
1533	// landPushAutoMergeConflictError) or some other failure.
1534	//
1535	// Caller responsibilities (already met by landPush):
1536	//   - The local target ref is at newTip and not yet pushed.
1537	//   - hasOrigin is true.
1538	func landPushAutoRecover(wd, targetBranch, newTip string, gitOps LandingGitOps) (recovered bool, mergeSHA string, err error) {
1539		dirtyBefore := dirtyWorktreePaths(wd)
1540		if fetchErr := gitOps.FetchBranch(wd, "origin", targetBranch); fetchErr != nil {
1541			return false, "", fmt.Errorf("auto-recovery fetch origin %s: %w", targetBranch, fetchErr)
1542		}
1543		originSHA, resolveErr := gitOps.ResolveRef(wd, "refs/remotes/origin/"+targetBranch)
1544		if resolveErr != nil {
1545			return false, "", fmt.Errorf("auto-recovery resolve origin/%s: %w", targetBranch, resolveErr)
1546		}
1547		if originSHA == newTip {
1548			// Origin is already at our tip — push must have failed for a reason
1549			// other than a non-fast-forward (auth, branch protection, large
1550			// blob, etc.). Recovery cannot help.
1551			return false, "", fmt.Errorf("origin already at local tip; push failure not recoverable by auto-merge")
1552		}
1553		tempWT, mkErr := os.MkdirTemp("", "ddx-push-recover-*")
1554		if mkErr != nil {
1555			return false, "", fmt.Errorf("auto-recovery temp worktree: %w", mkErr)
1556		}
1557		_ = os.RemoveAll(tempWT)
1558		if addErr := gitOps.AddWorktree(wd, tempWT, originSHA); addErr != nil {
1559			return false, "", fmt.Errorf("auto-recovery add worktree at %s: %w", originSHA, addErr)
1560		}
1561		defer func() { _ = gitOps.RemoveWorktree(wd, tempWT) }()
1562	
1563		mergeMsg := fmt.Sprintf("Merge origin/%s into %s after push race", targetBranch, targetBranch)
1564		if mergeErr := gitOps.MergeInto(tempWT, newTip, mergeMsg); mergeErr != nil {
1565			return false, "", &landPushAutoMergeConflictError{inner: mergeErr}
1566		}
1567		mergeSHA, headErr := gitOps.HeadRevAt(tempWT)
1568		if headErr != nil {
1569			return false, "", fmt.Errorf("auto-recovery read merge HEAD: %w", headErr)
1570		}
1571		targetRef := "refs/heads/" + targetBranch
1572		if updErr := gitOps.UpdateRefTo(wd, targetRef, mergeSHA, newTip); updErr != nil {
1573			return false, "", fmt.Errorf("auto-recovery advance %s to %s: %w", targetRef, mergeSHA, updErr)
1574		}
1575		syncWorkTreeToHeadGuarded(gitOps, wd, newTip, dirtyBefore, nil)
1576		if pushErr := gitOps.PushFFOnly(wd, "origin", mergeSHA, targetBranch); pushErr != nil {
1577			return false, "", fmt.Errorf("auto-recovery retry push: %w", pushErr)
1578		}
1579		return true, mergeSHA, nil
1580	}
1581	
1582	// LandConflictAutoRecover attempts a 3-way merge of a preserved iteration ref
1583	// onto the current target-branch tip using the ort strategy with -X ours. The
1584	// strategy resolves mechanical conflicts (positional drift from parallel beads)
1585	// by favouring the current tip's version of any conflicting sections while still
1586	// including the preserved iteration's non-conflicting changes in the merge
1587	// commit. If the merge succeeds the local target branch is advanced and the new
1588	// merge commit SHA is returned.
1589	//
1590	// A non-nil error means the ort merge failed (unresolvable content conflict or
1591	// git error) — the caller should escalate to a focused conflict-resolve agent.
1592	// The target branch is never modified on error.
1593	func LandConflictAutoRecover(wd, preserveRef string, gitOps LandingGitOps) (string, error) {
1594		dirtyBefore := dirtyWorktreePaths(wd)
1595		targetBranch, err := gitOps.CurrentBranch(wd)
1596		if err != nil {
1597			return "", fmt.Errorf("resolving target branch: %w", err)
1598		}
1599		targetRef := "refs/heads/" + targetBranch
1600	
1601		currentTip, err := gitOps.ResolveRef(wd, targetRef)
1602		if err != nil {
1603			return "", fmt.Errorf("resolving target tip %s: %w", targetRef, err)
1604		}
1605	
1606		iterSHA, err := gitOps.ResolveRef(wd, preserveRef)
1607		if err != nil {
1608			return "", fmt.Errorf("resolving preserved ref %s: %w", preserveRef, err)
1609		}
1610	
1611		tempWT, mkErr := os.MkdirTemp("", "ddx-conflict-recover-*")
1612		if mkErr != nil {
1613			return "", fmt.Errorf("creating temp worktree: %w", mkErr)
1614		}
1615		_ = os.RemoveAll(tempWT)
1616		if addErr := gitOps.AddWorktree(wd, tempWT, currentTip); addErr != nil {
1617			return "", fmt.Errorf("adding temp worktree at %s: %w", currentTip, addErr)
1618		}
1619		defer func() { _ = gitOps.RemoveWorktree(wd, tempWT) }()
1620	
1621		mergeMsg := fmt.Sprintf("Merge preserved iteration %s after base drift (ort -X ours)", preserveRef)
1622		out, mergeErr := internalgit.Command(
1623			context.Background(), tempWT,
1624			"-c", "user.name=ddx-land-coordinator",
1625			"-c", "user.email=coordinator@ddx.local",
1626			"merge", "--no-ff", "-s", "ort", "-X", "ours", "-m", mergeMsg, iterSHA,
1627		).CombinedOutput()
1628		if mergeErr != nil {
1629			_ = internalgit.Command(context.Background(), tempWT, "merge", "--abort").Run()
1630			return "", fmt.Errorf("ort merge: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), mergeErr)
1631		}
1632	
1633		mergeSHA, headErr := gitOps.HeadRevAt(tempWT)
1634		if headErr != nil {
1635			return "", fmt.Errorf("reading merge HEAD: %w", headErr)
1636		}
1637		if updErr := gitOps.UpdateRefTo(wd, targetRef, mergeSHA, currentTip); updErr != nil {
1638			return "", fmt.Errorf("advancing %s to %s: %w", targetRef, mergeSHA, updErr)
1639		}
1640		syncWorkTreeToHeadGuarded(gitOps, wd, currentTip, dirtyBefore, nil)
1641		return mergeSHA, nil
1642	}

1644	// shortAttempt returns a 10-char slug derived from attemptID for use in temp
1645	// branch names. When attemptID is empty, it returns the current timestamp.
1646	func shortAttempt(attemptID string) string {
1647		if attemptID != "" {
1648			if len(attemptID) > 16 {
1649				return attemptID[:16]
1650			}
1651			return attemptID
1652		}
1653		return NowFunc().UTC().Format("20060102T150405")
1654	}
1655	
1656	// ApplyLandResultToExecuteBeadResult maps a LandResult onto an
1657	// ExecuteBeadResult so the execute-bead loop sees the correct final status.
1658	// It is the coordinator-pattern replacement for ApplyLandingToResult.
1659	func ApplyLandResultToExecuteBeadResult(res *ExecuteBeadResult, land *LandResult) {
1660		if land == nil || res == nil {
1661			return
1662		}
1663		switch land.Status {
1664		case "landed":
1665			// Fast-forward or merge commit — either way the target branch now
1666			// contains the worker's result. ResultRev's own parent is still
1667			// BaseRev so replay fidelity is preserved.
1668			res.Outcome = "merged"
1669			res.Reason = ""
1670			res.PreserveRef = ""
1671			if land.Merged {
1672				res.Reason = "merged onto current tip"
1673			}
1674			if land.PushConflict {
1675				res.Reason = PushConflictReasonPrefix + " " + land.PushError
1676			} else if land.PushFailed {
1677				res.Reason = PushFailedReasonPrefix + " " + land.PushError
1678			}
1679			if land.CheckoutSyncDeferred {
1680				detail := "checkout_sync_deferred"
1681				if len(land.CheckoutSyncDeferredPaths) > 0 {
1682					detail += ": " + strings.Join(land.CheckoutSyncDeferredPaths, ", ")
1683				}
1684				if res.Reason == "" {
1685					res.Reason = detail
1686				} else {
1687					res.Reason += "; " + detail
1688				}
1689			}
1690			// NewTip reflects the ref actually on the target branch (either
1691			// ResultRev on the ff path or the merge commit SHA on the merge path).
1692			if land.NewTip != "" {
1693				// Preserve the implementation rev before rewriting the compat alias.
1694				if res.ImplementationRev == "" {
1695					res.ImplementationRev = res.ResultRev
1696				}
1697				res.LandedRev = land.NewTip
1698				res.ResultRev = land.NewTip // backwards-compat alias mirrors LandedRev
1699			}
1700		case "preserved":
1701			res.Outcome = "preserved"
1702			res.Reason = land.Reason
1703			res.PreserveRef = land.PreserveRef
1704		case "no-changes":
1705			// Only overwrite when the worker itself did not already report
1706			// a richer no-changes rationale.
1707			if res.Outcome == "" || res.Outcome == ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded {
1708				res.Outcome = "no-changes"
1709			}
1710			if res.Reason == "" {
1711				res.Reason = land.Reason
1712			}
1713		}
1714		// Re-classify loop-visible status from the landing outcome.
1715		reasonForStatus := res.Reason
1716		if land.Status == "preserved" {
1717			// Route preserve reasons through the land-conflict classifier so the
